macro:
I'll search for current market-moving news and today's economic calendar.I have enough. Let me verify the calendar for today with one focused search.The Schwab and CNBC sources already confirm today's schedule. I have what I need.

*U.S. cash equities are pre-open; drivers below describe the Monday, July 20 pre-market session (futures trading).*

TITLE: Chip selloff pushes SOX into bear market
BODY: A selloff in chipmakers drove the group into a bear market on worries the AI spending spree is harder to justify, with a key industry gauge sinking 20% from a record and posting its worst week since April 2025 — jolted by a breakthrough from Chinese AI startup Moonshot. Chipmakers traded higher in Monday's premarket to give U.S. equity markets a boost, but the sector remains the market's central swing factor into this week's mega-cap tech earnings.
IMPACT: high

TITLE: US-Iran strikes keep oil and inflation in focus
BODY: Stock futures rose Monday as oil prices fluctuated in response to the latest bout of military exchanges between the U.S. and Iran, with the U.S. completing its ninth consecutive day of strikes overnight. Investor sentiment improved by midmorning in London after an Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman lifted hopes for a diplomatic settlement, saying negotiations could be pursued based on national interests.
IMPACT: high

TITLE: Futures higher but direction is mixed
BODY: U.S. stock futures advanced Monday, with the SPDR S&P 500 ETF up 0.22% and the Invesco QQQ up 0.40% in premarket trading. Markets are pricing an 87.7% likelihood of the Fed leaving rates unchanged at July's meeting, with the 10-year Treasury yielding 4.55% and the two-year at 4.18%.
IMPACT: medium

TITLE: Housing data weighs on rate-sensitive names
BODY: Pending home sales dropped 5.4% in June and 30-year mortgage rates sit at 6.55%, keeping pressure on housing-related stocks and raising questions for banks, builders and real estate funds. The 10-year Treasury yield has slipped to about 4.5% after softer inflation readings, easing borrowing costs even as the debate over the Fed's next move continues.
IMPACT: medium

TITLE: Mega-cap earnings loom as season starts strong
BODY: Earnings season is off to a strong start; the big banks that kicked off Q2 reporting crushed expectations, and S&P 500 companies are expected to show quarterly earnings growth of greater than 20%. Alphabet and Tesla report this week and will give some idea of where the semis go next.
IMPACT: medium

CALENDAR: No major U.S. economic data releases are scheduled today. Schwab's investors' calendar notes July 20: no major earnings or data expected.

reads.market_condition: Pre-market Monday, and all three index proxies come in tinted bear off Friday's close with each opening as an inside bar against its prior day range. The scanner has no armed setups and no near misses on the board right now — the tape is coiled, waiting for a range to break rather than offering anything to act on. Prior-day highs and lows are the fences that matter.

reads.read: Down about half a percent below Friday's close and pressed against the very bottom of its prior-day range, with PDL 740.80 doubling as both the 1D and 1W magnet below and PDH 747.29 the ceiling above. The inside-bar open means neither fence has given way yet. A 15m close below 740.80 or back through 747.29 is what turns this coil into something the scanner can nominate.

Softest of the three, sitting just off the middle of a wide prior-day range that runs from PDL 686.76 up to PDH 702.30. The 4H magnet below at 694.22 sits between price and that daily floor, so there's air underneath before the range low. Nothing is armed; the inside-bar open keeps it in balance until one of those edges breaks on a closing basis.

Essentially flat and the only one still hugging its prior close, coiled tightly inside a narrow 291.64–296.13 range with the daily and weekly floor stacked at 291.64. This is the most compressed of the group and the least committed directionally. It stays a wait until price closes through either fence.
